subtitle: Agile Delivery · Healthcare · Continuous Improvement
hero_sub: Ten years of coordinating teams, removing obstacles, coaching toward self-organization, and producing measurable operating gains. In regulated environments. At scale.

the_case.need: Plan, coordinate, and direct project team activities
the_case.answer: Eptura: directed Agile delivery across ~10 distributed teams. A&E: coordinated ~10 concurrent product workstreams. Crocs: planned and directed multi-function teams through a full release lifecycle.
the_case.detail: Planning, coordinating, and directing is the core of every SM role I've held. At Eptura I owned delivery coordination across ~10 teams — PI planning cycles, sprint execution, cross-team dependency management, and leadership reporting. At Crocs I directed Engineering, QA, Change Management, and Business teams through a hard go-live deadline with no misses. At A&E I coordinated ~10 concurrent SVOD product workstreams simultaneously across a globally distributed team.

the_case.need: Identify and address obstacles hindering team progress
the_case.answer: Proactive impediment identification and removal is the documented pattern across every role — surfaced early, escalated with context, resolved before they stall delivery.
the_case.detail: At Eptura I managed cross-team dependencies and impediments across ~10 teams — escalating quickly when a blocker needed leadership attention and resolving the rest without escalation. At Understood.org I took over a distressed program in its final months and unblocked delivery by resetting scope, communication, and team dynamics simultaneously. At Crocs I maintained RAID logs and surfaced risks early enough to protect go-live.

the_case.need: Coach and mentor team members and stakeholders on Agile methodologies
the_case.answer: Eptura: 35%+ efficiency gain through coaching, not just process. Underline: established Agile from scratch in an organization that had never used it. A&E: two-year cultural transformation from Post-it notes to structured delivery.
the_case.detail: Coaching that produces measurable operating gains is the track record. At Eptura I coached ~10 teams through varying maturity levels, mentored junior Scrum Masters, and spearheaded Agile adoption in a Marketing department with zero prior practice. At Underline I sold Scrum to skeptical leadership, recruited and ran a distributed development team, and delivered on time in 16 weeks. The efficiency gains at Eptura did not come from process enforcement — they came from sustained behavioral coaching.

the_case.need: Support Product Owner in product backlog management and prioritization
the_case.answer: PO partnership is the consistent pattern: Eptura (10 POs, execution-ready backlogs), Crocs (scope and prioritization governance), Paperless Post (Optimizely-tied backlog outcomes), A&E (roadmap alignment).
the_case.detail: I partner with Product Owners on more than grooming — I coach them on what makes a story execution-ready vs. aspirational, enforce Definition of Ready/Done discipline, and align backlog priorities to delivery capacity and business objectives. At Paperless Post I tied Jira backlog management directly to Optimizely A/B testing outcomes so delivery was connected to measurable product results.

the_case.need: Develop strategies to monitor and measure team progress
the_case.answer: Eptura: built standardized BI dashboards for ~10 teams in ADO, Jira, and Smartsheet. Crocs: real-time release readiness dashboards. A&E: Jira metrics infrastructure built from scratch for all teams.
the_case.detail: I build the monitoring and measurement infrastructure — not just use it. At Eptura I created the standardized delivery health dashboards (velocity, burndown, cycle time, team health) that became the PMO standard across all ~10 teams. At Crocs I built Release Radar, a real-time tracking framework that surfaced cross-environment dependencies before they threatened go-live. At A&E I built Jira from scratch for all teams after they were tracking delivery on Post-it notes.

the_case.answer: 35%+ efficiency gain at Eptura through retrospective-driven improvement. 20% sprint carryover reduction. Agile adoption from scratch at Underline and in the Marketing department at Eptura. Cultural transformation at A&E over two years.
the_case.detail: Self-organization is the goal; the ceremonies and coaching are the mechanism. At Eptura I drove continuous improvement through structured retrospective-to-action cycles, process standardization, and data-driven coaching interventions. The 35%+ efficiency gain and 20% carryover reduction are outcomes of teams that improved their own delivery, not just teams that followed a process I imposed.

the_case.need: Manage multiple assignments concurrently in a fast-paced environment
the_case.answer: Managing multiple concurrent teams and workstreams is the standard operating mode: Eptura (~10 teams), A&E (~10 workstreams), Crocs (multi-function concurrent tracks), Paperless Post (2 teams simultaneously).
the_case.detail: I have never held a role that managed a single team. The standard has been 2-10 concurrent teams or workstreams across every significant engagement. At Eptura I owned delivery coordination across ~10 teams while maintaining individual coaching relationships with each PO and tech lead. At A&E I shipped ~10 concurrent workstreams simultaneously while holding high-visibility, externally committed launch windows.

the_stack.headline: The delivery ecosystem.
the_stack.subhead: The tools and practices that make progress visible to leadership.

nodes.label: Jira & Jira AI
nodes.desc: Primary delivery tracking — sprint boards, workflows, velocity dashboards, burndown, team health, Jira AI for backlog synthesis and reporting
nodes.pm_role: Built Jira from scratch at A&E (from Post-it notes). Standardized Jira workflows and dashboards across ~10 teams at Eptura. Used Jira AI at Crocs to reduce reporting overhead ~30% while improving output quality.

nodes.label: Azure DevOps (ADO)
nodes.desc: Enterprise delivery tracking and release management — board configuration, sprint dashboards, delivery health reporting
nodes.pm_role: Maintained ADO delivery dashboards at Eptura and Crocs; used alongside Jira for cross-platform leadership visibility.

nodes.label: Smartsheet
nodes.desc: Program-level reporting, capacity planning, leadership dashboards, dependency tracking
nodes.pm_role: Built leadership-level delivery health reports at Eptura synthesizing across all ~10 teams into a single view for executive stakeholders.

nodes.label: Microsoft Copilot
nodes.desc: AI-assisted stakeholder communication, meeting synthesis, and administrative overhead reduction
nodes.pm_role: Embedded at Crocs — reduced administrative overhead ~30% while improving the quality and timeliness of leadership reporting.

nodes.label: Confluence
nodes.desc: Team documentation, working agreements, retrospective tracking, Definition of Done
nodes.pm_role: Standardized Jira/Confluence configuration across teams so delivery artifacts are organized, current, and accessible without a PM intermediary.

when_things_break.scenario: Sprint carryover is increasing across three consecutive sprints
response: Diagnose before acting: capacity issue, story readiness issue, or accountability issue?
Pull Jira burndown and story completion data — find the pattern, not just the symptom
Partner with PO on backlog readiness — are stories entering sprint execution-ready?
Retrospective with explicit framing: 'Three sprints of this — what changed and what haven't we named?'
Introduce WIP limits or readiness gates if context-switching or unready stories are the driver

when_things_break.scenario: Team is going through the motions — ceremonies happening, nothing improving
response: Name the pattern directly: 'We've been doing this for N sprints and nothing has changed'
Change the retrospective format completely — if the same structure produces no results, the structure is the problem
1:1s with team members before the next ceremony — not after
Introduce a concrete, small experiment: one change, measurable in one sprint
If the team cannot identify what needs to change, that IS the finding — escalate to leadership

when_things_break.scenario: Stakeholder introduces scope change mid-sprint with executive pressure behind it
response: Stop the conversation — assess actual impact before responding to the pressure
Map the trade-off explicitly: what comes out if this goes in, or does the sprint goal change?
Bring the trade-off to Product Owner for the decision — not the stakeholder
Communicate the decision to all parties with the reasoning documented
Post-mortem: what process gap allowed a mid-sprint scope request to get executive backing?

how_i_run_a_room: Sprint planning ends with committed goals and owned action items — not aspirational lists
Retrospectives produce at least one measurable improvement per sprint, tracked to resolution
Obstacles are identified and addressed within 24 hours — leadership hears about the ones that need them
Business intelligence dashboards give leadership an honest picture before they ask for one
PO partnership is continuous — I know what is in the next two sprints and why
Team coaching is individualized, not uniform — different maturity levels get different approaches
Multiple concurrent teams stay aligned without each requiring a separate management layer

track_record.role: Agile Coach & Senior Scrum Master — Agile PMO

35%+ delivery efficiency gain; 20% sprint carryover reduction — measurable operating gains

Healthcare-adjacent, regulated environment — WCAG 2.1 AA compliance as core delivery requirement
Distressed program recovery: took over in final months, delivered on time with no critical regressions
Globally distributed team across 12+ countries — coordination and progress monitoring at scale
Compliance-conscious delivery discipline directly applicable to healthcare environment
Planned and directed multi-function teams through a full release lifecycle under hard deadline
Built real-time delivery visibility dashboards (Release Radar) for leadership
Jira AI + Copilot reduced administrative overhead ~30%; protected on-time go-live
SOX-compliant change control in a regulated, governance-conscious environment
Coordinated ~10 concurrent SVOD workstreams (Web, iOS, tvOS, Android, Roku) simultaneously

Two-year cultural transformation producing measurable improvement in execution quality

before: Teams are meeting but not improving — ceremonies without measurable operating gains
Progress monitoring is reactive — leadership only hears about problems after they've impacted delivery
Multiple concurrent teams require constant senior management oversight to stay aligned
Coaching is inconsistent — some teams mature, others stay stuck
after: Retrospectives produce documented, tracked improvements every sprint
Business intelligence dashboards give leadership an honest view before they ask for one
Multiple concurrent teams stay aligned without requiring a separate management layer for each
Coaching is individualized to maturity level and produces measurable behavioral change

the_ramp.deliverable: Audit current team health, metrics, and delivery cadence across all assigned teams. Meet every team member, PO, and key stakeholder. No changes yet — understand the current state first.
Facilitate first full ceremony cycle. Identify top 3 obstacles and process gaps per team. Propose first metrics improvement to leadership.
Business intelligence dashboards baseline established. First retrospective with documented, tracked output. Direct coaching conversations underway where needed.
Running all assigned teams cleanly. Leadership has honest visibility into progress, risk, and productivity. First measurable operating gain documented.
